description: This plot displays the variations of Vickers hardness (Hᵥ, unit: GPa, left Y-axis, red square curve) and Debye temperature (Θ_D, unit: K, right Y-axis, blue square curve) of Fe₁₋ₓMnₓAl alloys with Mn content ranging from 0 to 1, calculated via density functional theory based first-principles methods. The two properties show highly consistent changing trends: both decrease continuously as Mn content increases from 0 to 0.5, reaching their minimum values (Hᵥ ~6.7 GPa, Θ_D ~530 K) at Mn content of 0.5, then increase from Mn=0.5 to 0.8 to a secondary peak (Hᵥ ~9.8 GPa, Θ_D ~570 K), and finally drop to the lowest values for MnAl (x=1, Hᵥ ~5 GPa, Θ_D ~502 K). FeAl (x=0) has the highest Hᵥ (~16.5 GPa) and Θ_D (~650 K), indicating the strongest covalent bonding, while MnAl shows the strongest metallic character. This figure supports that bonding nature and related properties of Fe-Mn-Al alloys are highly Mn-content dependent.
